titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Loren and Stanley Disney Family Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 18, 2021 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Loren and Stanley Disney Family Papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

persname.part: Disney, Dorothy Cameron (Dorothy Cameron Disney MacKaye)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Book Shop LLC on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund For Western Americana, 2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, Writings and Personal Papers Related to Stanley E. Disney, 1898-1989. II. Correspondence, Writings, and Personal Papers Related to the Disney family, 1924-1965. III. Correspondence and Writings related to Loren G. Disney, 1896-1900.

bioghist.head: The Loren and Stanley Disney Family
bioghist.p: Loren G. Disney (1876-1938) was born in Leavenworth, KS and served in the Spanish-American War. He was a lawyer, judge, and Republican political figure in Oklahoma during its transition from territory to statehood. Disney's writings document the treatment of Indigenous peoples and African Americans in Oklahoma, along with political happenings, his experiences as a soldier, and other elements of his life.

Stanley Eugene Disney (1910-1985) was born in Muskogee, Oklahoma. He is the son of Loren G Disney and married Frances Higbee Martin in 1936. Disney attended Oklahoma University and George Washington University Law School. He worked as an attorney for the United States Department of Justice from 1929-1942, then served in World War II before returning to the Department of Justice from 1948-1950. Disney worked as Attorney for Allied High Commission in Germany through 1954, then returned to the US Justice Department from 1955 until his retirement in 1975.

Frances Higbee Martin Disney (1910-1993) was born in Muskogee, Oklahoma, attended the University of Alabama, and graduated from National Park Seminary in Washington, D.C. in 1910, and married Stanley E. Disney in 1936. She and Disney adopted nine children during the course of their marriage.

Dorothy Cameron Disney MacKaye (1903-1992) was a mystery writer and journalist. Under a byline of Dorothy Cameron Disney, she published nine novels. Her column, "Can This Marriage Be Saved," ran in "Ladies' Home Journal" for thirty years.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Loren and Stanley Disney Family Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Loren and Stanley Disney Family Papers, which span from 1896 to 1989, contain correspondence, diaries, writings, photographs, clippings, ephemera, and other papers created by or related to Loren G. Disney, his children Stanley E. Disney and Dorothy Cameron Disney MacKaye, and Stanley E. Disney's wife, Frances Higbee Martin Disney.

An unpublished, typescript memoir, "Political Recollections of L. G. Disney," describes Loren Disney's life in Oklahoma politics and government from 1900 into the 1920s including his work for the Dawes Commission in Muskogee; his collaboration with Edward Perry, the Canadian-born husband of Carrie LeFlore, the daughter of Choctaw leader Forbis LeFlore; and his participation in Alice Mary Robertson's successful 1920 campaign for election to the United States House of Representatives. The bulk of the papers document the personal lives of Stanley E. Disney and his wife, Frances Higbee Martin Disney. Stanley Disney's papers related to his service as Deputy chief and chief, Decartelization and Deconcentration Division, United States Office of Military Government in Germany, 1950-1955 were acquired by the Hoover Institute, Stanford University, in 1986. Stanley Disney's correspondence includes a run of letters from his sister, author Dorothy Cameron Disney MacKaye.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Loren and Stanley Disney Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

geogname.part: Commission to the Five Civilized Tribes

did.unittitle: Correspondence, writings and personal papers related to Stanley E. Disney

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence sent to Stanley E. Disney, much of it from his wife Frances Higbee Martin Disney. Also contains diaries, writings, clippings, ephemera, and personal papers created by or related to Stanley E. Disney.

did.unittitle: Letters sent to Stanley E. Disney
Letters sent to Stanley E. Disney
Letters sent to Stanley E. Disney
Letters sent to Stanley E. Disney
Letters sent to Stanley E. Disney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily contains numerous notes from Frances Disney, Stanley E. Disney's wife, many of which document marital discord and divorce. Also includes a small number of notes and cards from others.

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Writings of Stanley Disney for Frances Disney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume includes writings, clippings, copyright paperwork, and a letter tucked inside the cover.

did.unittitle: Letter to Stanley E. Disney's parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album includes loose and adhered photographs, many of which depict military life.

did.unittitle: Passes to United States Senate Chamber

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes programs, booklets, and other pieces of printed material collected by Stanley E. Disney.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, writings, and personal papers related to the Disney family

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains extensive correspondence sent to Disney family members, much of it sent to Frances Higbee Martin Disney. Also contains diaries, ephemera, and personal papers created by or related to Disney family members.

did.unittitle: Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily contains letters sent to Frances Martin Disney, but also includes several possibly written by Frances Martin Disney.

did.unittitle: Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney
Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney
Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney
Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney
Letters sent to Frances Martin Disney
Correspondence from Frances Martin Disney's mother
Correspondence from Stanley E. Disney's mother

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This notebook contains writings by Frances Higbee Martin, who later married Stanley E. Disney.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and writings related to Loren G Disney

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains writings, diaries, and correspondence created by Loren G Disney. Much of the writing documents Disney's experiences as a soldier during the Spanish-American War and his life and work in Oklahoma.

did.unittitle: Political Recollections of L. G. Disney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains bound volume of typescript writings by Loren G. Disney.
